task_updates.executor_notes: Added pure capacity planning modules `gpu_orchestrator/control/capacity_types.py` and `gpu_orchestrator/control/capacity_plan.py`. The new types are frozen dataclasses for observed pool state, route demand, worker snapshots, intent records, actions, plans, admitted plans, and result counters, with a `Clock` protocol and `SystemClock`. Implemented `build_capacity_plan()` as a pure pool-scoped function using exactly `min(max_active_gpus, max(min_active_gpus, busy_count + eligible_queued_total, busy_count + max(machines_to_keep_idle, 1)))`; route backoff only affects `eligible_queued_total` and suppressed spawn actions, while min/max/buffer apply once at pool level. The plan treats non-route-stale active and spawning workers as effective capacity, excludes route-stale workers, allocates spawn budget to route demand before `__pool_floor__`, emits idle-active termination only for idle candidates, and gates pending-spawn cancellation through wall-clock hysteresis or expired `valid_until`. Added `tests/gpu_orchestrator/control/test_capacity_plan.py`. Verified with `python -m pytest tests/gpu_orchestrator/control/test_capacity_plan.py -q`, `python -m pytest tests/gpu_orchestrator/control -q`, `python -m py_compile gpu_orchestrator/control/capacity_types.py gpu_orchestrator/control/capacity_plan.py`, and the scaling/route-stale targeted test module command. Full suite was run with `python -m pytest --tb=no -q --no-header`; it failed only at the already observed `tests/test_module_import_coverage.py::test_runpod_cutover_modules_import_at_runtime` because `scripts.spot_instance_lifetime_test` is missing, unrelated to T3.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Yes. Planning is pure and clock-injected, uses frozen input/output dataclasses, applies one pool-level desired formula, and applies route backoff only by reducing eligible queued demand and recording suppressed spawn actions. Min, max, and idle buffer are not multiplied by route.
